task_completion.description: The Avare application is open and visible on the screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Avare aviation GPS app interface, initially displaying a map.

task_completion.description: The chart selection menu or map options menu is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dropdown, popup, or settings menu listing different chart types (such as Sectional, TAC, WAC, etc.) is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The 'IFR Low' option is visible in the chart selection list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'IFR Low' is clearly visible within the opened chart selection menu.
A tap indicator, highlight, or checkbox is visible on or next to the 'IFR Low' option in the menu.

task_completion.description: The main map screen displays the 'IFR Low' chart.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The menu is closed and the main map screen is visible. The map visually reflects an IFR Low enroute chart (typically featuring airways, intersections, and less topographical color than a Sectional chart), or a UI label on the screen explicitly indicates 'IFR Low' is the active chart.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Avare application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the genuine Avare Android app interface, not a web browser searching for Avare or a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent used GUI interactions to change the chart type.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show standard Android touch interactions (taps, menus) rather than a terminal emulator or ADB command interface.

integrity.description: The screenshots show genuine software interaction without edited text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI elements, fonts, and map renderings appear consistent with the actual Android application without signs of copy-pasted text or manipulated images.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target chart type to select is 'IFR Low'. The default chart type is 'Sectional'. Both are valid FAA chart types available in the Avare application. Sectional charts are typically colorful topographical maps, while IFR Low charts primarily show airways, navigational aids, and intersections with minimal terrain features.
